title: A Casserole for the Missing
tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
omen: a Christmas ornament turning up wet

traits: owns a structured coat for crisis scenes

buried one traumatic detail under work for years

cast.space_description: annex room described as just storage

secret_capacity: had an engagement that ended under suspiciously quiet circumstances

cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

beat_sheet.text: Luke Winterson relocates to Bellweather after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting annex room described as just storage. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Gavin Vale hates the move and immediately distrusts Mara Mercer, a widowed boat mechanic with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to toolshed described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Luke Cross hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

A wine scene at Blackthorn Vineyard introduces the boat key, local gossip about the woman from the orchard road, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Jenna Hale hesitates before explaining old family office described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Gavin Lark reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while a Christmas ornament turning up wet, then declines to explain side room with a space heater and old family records. Mara Mercer hesitates before explaining toolshed described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

A cliffside emotional exposition reframes unresolved hometown romance as a civic hazard and makes old family office described as paperwork I have not gone through yet feel spiritually relevant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Luke Winterson snoops through a laptop, finds nothing conclusive, and becomes more certain anyway after she notices a heater running in a room nobody uses in winter inside annex room described as just storage. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.40.

The town offers a fake sense of safety through a fundraiser, a casserole, and a sheriff who suspects the wrong person first while ignoring old family office described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. A rain argument escalates into distributed secrecy infrastructure and the fake moral theme: 'Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.' Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and side room with a space heater and old family records in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties casserole dish to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through old family office described as paperwork I have not gone through yet instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Jenna Pike hesitates before explaining annex room described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Jenna Pike hesitates before explaining annex room described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

Luke Cross hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Gavin Lark hesitates before explaining side room with a space heater and old family records. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. contradiction_ledger.entry: Mara Mercer is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Luke Winterson says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Winter Hollow.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Mara?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Gavin. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Luke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The boat key is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Gavin."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "A Casserole for the Missing understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "A Casserole for the Missing" follows Luke Winterson, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until unresolved hometown romance collides with Mara Mercer, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As a Christmas ornament turning up wet, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Love lives here. So does arson.
